title: When Does a Man Die?
description: On pages 13–16, this part focuses on when does a man die?, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: When Does a Man Die?. Hiriluk faces Wapol’s deception and declares that people live on through their dreams before his final sacrifice. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Hiriluk
dialogue.text: Don't you realize!?

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: The only man who wished to save this country died just now!

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: While others have long given up on this country and thought it hopeless,

dialogue.speaker: Twenty Doctors
dialogue.text: There was one kind doctor who surprisingly endeavoured to save it! But that man has now persisted!

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: So what if one quack doctor died!?

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: If you're going to apologize, I suggest you do it now, Dalton.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I now see clearly the fate of this kingdom...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: inevitable ruin.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: What!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: As long as we continue to trample the people beneath our feet, this country can never be fixed!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: No matter how advanced the doctors of this country and their medicine are,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: This country's doomed fate shall not change!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #3
dialogue.text: Because there is no cure for a fool!

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: How dare you...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: You should be well aware of what happens to those who truly anger me...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #4
dialogue.text: right, dalton...?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #5
dialogue.text: Please!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #6
dialogue.text: Teach me to become a doctor!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'll become the panacea myself!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'll become a doctor that can cure anything!

dialogue.speaker: Chess
dialogue.text: Because...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Because there are no incurable diseases,

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: In this whole world!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #7
dialogue.text: Dottress...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #8
dialogue.text: That's what you're to address me from now on...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Just one little "sorry" and I'll be happy to forgive you, Dalton. Mahahaha!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I won't die... I won't!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: I'll inherit the will of the former king...!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: Until I can bring peace to this country again, I refuse to die!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: mahahahaha! Oh why oh why am I surrounded by a bunch of dumb hippos!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: This country is mine and mine to rule! The King is above reproach!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #2
dialogue.text: Ahhhhaha!

summary: This part highlights when does a man die? and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
